goal.summary: Continue P1 Tau hardening by making DAG fail_closed_on invariants executable and project-agent-readable.

commands_run.command: uv run ruff check --select F,I src/tau_coding/project_dag.py tests/test_project_dag.py
uv run pytest tests/test_project_dag.py tests/test_generic_dag.py tests/test_generic_provider_adapter.py -q

commands_run.result: No whitespace errors reported for tracked diffs

what_changed: Added tau.fail_closed_registry.v1 metadata for known DAG fail-closed invariant codes.
DAG contract validation now rejects unknown fail_closed_on codes before dispatch.
Unknown fail_closed_on codes are returned through tau.dag_error.v1 course-correction JSON from the CLI failure path.
The committed DAG runtime also includes active context propagation and bounded-ready-queue stress coverage already present in the worktree and covered by tests.
proves: Tau exposes a deterministic fail-closed invariant registry payload for DAG authors.
Tau rejects a DAG contract containing an unknown fail_closed_on value such as nebulous_goal.
The rejection is packaged as a project-agent-readable tau.dag_error.v1 payload and recommends goal-guardian repair.

The project DAG focused suite passes with creator/reviewer, YAML, context propagation, ready-queue concurrency, timeout retry, non-JSON retry, max-retry block, provider branch block, malformed contract error, missing command spec error, missing evidence, reviewer target mismatch, cycle block, mutating-node block, and fail_closed_on registry coverage.
does_not_prove: Runtime-wide enforcement for every possible fail_closed_on code beyond currently implemented validators.
Provider branch locks, Memory sync, GitHub mutation, browser UI proof, or full WebGPT roadmap completion.
That future adaptive DAG expansion proposals are safe to apply automatically.

next_recommended_route.reason: Continue P1 with evidence manifests/validators or provider work-order enforcement across runtime entrypoints.
